function.name: pinchtab_health
function.description: Check if the PinchTab browser automation server is reachable and healthy. Call this first before using any other pinchtab tools to verify the connection. Returns server information including version, uptime, and active instances.

function.name: pinchtab_list_instances
function.description: List all browser instances managed by PinchTab. Each instance represents a Chrome browser process. Returns instance IDs, profiles, and status — use this to discover which instances are available before opening tabs.

function.name: pinchtab_new_tab
function.description: Open a new browser tab in a PinchTab instance. Optionally navigate to a URL immediately. Returns the new tab's ID which you'll need for all subsequent tab operations (navigate, snapshot, click, etc.). The instance_id is optional — when omitted, the server auto-routes to the appropriate instance.

instance_id.description: Optional ID of the browser instance to create the tab in. Use pinchtab_list_instances to find available instances. When omitted, the server auto-routes.

url.description: Optional URL to navigate the new tab to immediately after creation.

function.name: pinchtab_navigate
function.description: Navigate a browser tab to a URL. The tab must already exist (created via pinchtab_new_tab). URLs are safety-checked — file://, chrome://, javascript:, and internal/localhost addresses are blocked.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to navigate.

url.description: The URL to navigate to. Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL.

function.name: pinchtab_snapshot
function.description: Get the accessibility tree snapshot of a browser tab. This is the recommended way to inspect page content — the accessibility tree is a structured representation of all elements with their roles, labels, and states. It is 5-13x more token-efficient than screenshots, making it ideal for LLM-based analysis. Use this to read page content, find elements to interact with, or understand page structure.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to snapshot.

function.name: pinchtab_get_text
function.description: Extract plain text content from a browser tab. Returns all visible text on the page as a single string. Use this when you need raw text content without the structure of the accessibility tree. For structured element inspection, prefer pinchtab_snapshot.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to extract text from.

function.name: pinchtab_click
function.description: Click on an element in a browser tab. The element can be identified by its `ref` from pinchtab_snapshot (e.g. 'e5', 'e21') or by a CSS selector (e.g. '#submit-btn'). Do NOT use the `nodeId` field as a selector. Use this to interact with buttons, links, checkboxes, and other clickable elements.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to click in.

selector.description: The `ref` value from pinchtab_snapshot (e.g. 'e5', 'e21') or a CSS selector (e.g. '#submit-btn', '.nav-link'). IMPORTANT: use the `ref` field, NOT the `nodeId` field from snapshot results.

function.name: pinchtab_type
function.description: Type text into an input element in a browser tab. Use this to fill form fields, search boxes, and text areas. The target element is identified by its `ref` from pinchtab_snapshot (e.g. 'e21') or by a CSS selector.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to type in.

selector.description: The `ref` value from pinchtab_snapshot (e.g. 'e21') or a CSS selector. IMPORTANT: use the `ref` field, NOT the `nodeId` field from snapshot results.

text.description: The text to type into the element.

function.name: pinchtab_screenshot
function.description: Take a screenshot of a browser tab. Returns a base64-encoded image of the current page viewport (or the full scrollable page if full_page=true). Note: screenshots are expensive in token consumption — prefer pinchtab_snapshot for content analysis when visuals aren't required.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to screenshot.

full_page.description: If true, capture the full scrollable page instead of just the visible viewport. Default: false.

function.name: pinchtab_eval
function.description: Evaluate a JavaScript expression in a browser tab. The expression runs in the page context and its return value is serialized and returned. Use this to execute custom JavaScript, inject cookies via document.cookie, inspect the DOM, or trigger page-side logic. Requires a tab_id and a valid JavaScript expression.

tab_id.description: ID of the tab to evaluate the expression in.

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. For cookie injection, use: document.cookie = 'key=value; domain=.example.com; path=/'. The return value of the expression is included in the result.
